title: pholidota imbricata var. henryi
intro: Pholidota imbricata var. henryi, also known as Henry's stinkwood, is a plant species within the family Stemonuraceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Henry's stinkwood include its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent infections. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Henry's stinkwood are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
